our_argument_summary: Our review argues: (1) Schumann suppression during G3+ storms is standard ionospheric plasma physics (particle precipitation → increased D-region conductivity → altered Q-factor), with no dome equivalent mechanism; (2) the dome's cavity formula H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) km predicts a baseline ~22 Hz (H_eff ≈ 3,400 km), not 7.83 Hz, so claiming to explain storm-time perturbations around a wrong baseline is incoherent; (3) no dome formula predicts the observed ~0.3 Hz frequency shift or ~30% amplitude reduction. Verdict: Self-Contradicted. Cross-references Section 2.1 (Schumann self-contradiction).

cycle_2_focus.cycle_1_fixes_applied: Partially. The Cycle 1 moderate hole about the aetheric medium hand-wave was addressed — the text now preemptively notes that no dome formula predicts the ~0.3 Hz shift or ~30% amplitude reduction. However, three other Cycle 1 recommendations were NOT applied: (1) the citation is still the general Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 chapter rather than the more targeted Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999; (2) the '~22 Hz' figure still uses H_eff ≈ 3,400 km without specifying the averaging method; (3) the stronger arguments about copper firmament contradiction and WIN-038 stability tension were not added.
cycle_2_focus.text_change_summary: detail_evidence grew significantly since Cycle 1 — from a shorter argument to 1325 chars. The aetheric medium preemption and specific quantitative claims (~0.3 Hz shift, ~30% amplitude reduction) were added. Cross-reference updated to V6 numbering (Section 2.1). These are positive improvements.
cycle_2_focus.v6_restructure_compliance: Cross-reference correctly uses V6 numbering: 'Section 2.1' with href='#part2' and onclick='showTab("selftest")'. This is correct — old Section 4.5.1 is now Section 2.1 in the Self-Contradictions tab.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: The dome's live power dashboard lists Domain 9 as 'Schumann Amplitude Suppression' with falsification criterion: 'G3+ storm (Kp≥7) does NOT cause >30% amplitude drop within 6 hours.' Current status: 'Unscored — no G3+ storm observed.' The additional domains section mentions 'Daily Kp–SR suppression — >30% amplitude drop required on G1+ storm.' The monitoring code (no longer available as raw .py files in this workspace) had these as pass=None per Cycle 1 analysis. The key finding: the dome's own monitoring infrastructure has NEVER scored this WIN because no G3+ storm occurred during the monitoring window. WIN-061 is listed as 'CONFIRMED' in the dome's wins list despite the monitoring system reporting 'Unscored.'
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither hardcoded nor computed — the domain exists as a conditional placeholder that has never triggered. The falsification test (>30% amplitude drop during G3+) has never been evaluated because no qualifying storm event was observed. This is structurally different from the hardcoded WINs (where pred=obs is baked in) — here, the test literally never ran.
data_sources_used: HeartMath GCI API (intended, but never triggered)
NOAA Kp index (storm trigger condition, never met threshold)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Double escape: (1) test only activates during G3+ storms (rare events), (2) monitoring status is 'Unscored' because no qualifying storm was observed. The WIN is claimed as confirmed without the monitoring system ever having evaluated it.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our WIN-061 text does not make specific code claims — the critique is at the physics level. The code analysis revealing 'Unscored' status is an additional untapped critique: the dome claims confirmation for a test that has never been run.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Domain 9 in the dome's live power dashboard is 'Schumann Amplitude Suppression' with status 'Unscored — no G3+ storm observed.' The falsification criterion (G3+ storm → >30% amplitude drop within 6 hours) has never been evaluated. Per Cycle 1 analysis, the corresponding monitor.py domains (#16 and #30) had pass=None. Functionally 'none' — the domain exists as a stub but performs no monitoring.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Schumann suppression during geomagnetic storms is textbook ionospheric physics documented since the 1960s. Mechanism: storm-time particle precipitation → increased D-region electron density → higher conductivity → altered cavity Q-factor → amplitude suppression and frequency shift. Schlegel & Füllekrug (1999, JGR), Nickolaenko et al. (2004). The dome renames the ionospheric conductivity mechanism as 'aetheric perturbation' but predicts no different numerical outcome.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Schumann perturbations during geomagnetic storms measured and published since the 1960s-70s. Systematic studies by Sentman (1990), Schlegel & Füllekrug (1999), Nickolaenko et al. (2004) all predate the dome model. WIN-061 is new in V51.0 (2026), adopting a 50+ year old observation as a 'confirmed prediction.'
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome formula connects storm-time aetheric perturbations to specific amplitude/frequency changes. The dome provides no derivation from its geometry for suppression magnitude, timing, or frequency shift. The claim simply asserts the observation 'matches the dome model' without specifying what the model actually predicts quantitatively.

holes_found.description: Cycle 1 finding still open: The citation (doi.org/10.1007/978-3-662-03137-4_11) points to a general Schumann resonance reference (Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002), not specifically to storm-time suppression. While valid, it's imprecise. A dome defender who checks the citation finds a general cavity physics chapter, not the specific storm-suppression evidence our text relies on.
holes_found.recommendation: Add Schlegel & Füllekrug (1999, doi:10.1029/1999JA900056) as an additional or replacement citation — this specifically documents Schumann frequency and amplitude changes during geomagnetic storms.

holes_found.description: The 'Unscored' status from the dome's own monitoring dashboard is a powerful untapped argument. The dome lists WIN-061 as 'CONFIRMED' in its wins table, but Domain 9 in the live power dashboard says 'Unscored — no G3+ storm observed.' This is a direct inconsistency in the dome's own presentation: how can a prediction be 'confirmed' when the test the dome itself defined has never been run? This should be mentioned in our review.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to detail_evidence: 'The dome's own Live Power Dashboard lists this domain as "Unscored — no G3+ storm observed," meaning the dome's self-defined falsification test has never been evaluated — yet the prediction is counted as confirmed.'

holes_found.description: Cycle 1 finding still open: The '~22 Hz' claim uses H_eff ≈ 3,400 km (simple linear average of H(r)), but doesn't specify the averaging method. Quantitative verification shows the simple linear average gives H_eff = 3,316 km → f ≈ 22.6 Hz. The area-weighted average gives H_eff = 2,135 km → f ≈ 35.1 Hz. The pole-only height gives f ≈ 8.8 Hz. All exceed 7.83 Hz, but the range is wide. This is consistent with WIN-002, WIN-029, and WIN-038, which all use the same ~22 Hz figure.
holes_found.recommendation: No individual WIN change needed — this should be standardized across all Schumann WINs (002, 029, 038, 061) simultaneously. If standardized, use: 'The dome's cavity predicts baseline frequencies of 8.8–35 Hz depending on the effective height, all above the observed 7.83 Hz.' The ~22 Hz figure (using simple linear average) is the most conservative non-pole estimate and defensible as-is.

holes_found.description: The stronger argument about copper firmament contradiction (WIN-034 vs WIN-061) identified in Cycle 1 was not added. If the firmament is copper (WIN-034), its conductivity is constant at ~6×10⁷ S/m regardless of geomagnetic storms. Schumann suppression requires variable boundary conductivity (the ionosphere). A copper dome should NOT show storm-time Schumann suppression. This is a clean internal contradiction that strengthens the Self-Contradicted verdict.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider adding: 'WIN-034 claims the firmament is copper/bronze — but copper's conductivity (~6×10⁷ S/m) is constant and immune to particle precipitation. Schumann suppression requires a boundary whose conductivity changes during storms (the ionosphere). A copper dome predicts no suppression.' This would be a concise addition to detail_extra.

stronger_arguments.description: The WIN-038 tension remains the strongest unexploited argument. WIN-038 claims Schumann is stable at 7.83 ±0.3 Hz. WIN-061 claims storm-time suppression (involving shifts of 0.3–1.0 Hz). During G3+ events, the frequency shift can exceed ±0.3 Hz, violating WIN-038's stability window. The dome counts both stability and instability as confirmed — a logical contradiction. This is not currently mentioned in WIN-061's text.

kernel_of_truth.description: Schumann suppression during G3+ geomagnetic storms is real. Amplitude drops of 20–40% and frequency shifts of ~0.3–1.0 Hz are well-documented. The dome model correctly identifies the phenomenon as genuine.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The phenomenon is entirely explained by standard ionospheric physics (D-region conductivity changes from particle precipitation). The dome adds no predictive content — no formula, no mechanism, no numerical prediction. The baseline frequency is wrong (dome predicts 8.8–35 Hz, observed 7.83 Hz). The dome's own monitoring has never scored this WIN ('Unscored — no G3+ storm observed'). And the dome's firmament material (copper, WIN-034) predicts the suppression shouldn't happen.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 2 re-review of WIN-061. The Self-Contradicted verdict is well-supported and the text has improved since Cycle 1 with the addition of preemptive arguments against the aetheric medium hand-wave.

**Cycle 1 Fix Status:**
- APPLIED: Aetheric medium preemption — the text now addresses why 'the aetheric medium is perturbed' is inadequate (no formula predicts specific ~0.3 Hz shift or ~30% amplitude reduction). This is a significant improvement.
- NOT APPLIED: Citation still general rather than storm-specific.
- NOT APPLIED: H_eff averaging method not specified (but consistent across all Schumann WINs).
- NOT APPLIED: Copper firmament and WIN-038 stability contradictions not added.

**Quantitative Verification (Cycle 2):**
Using the dome's H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) with R = 20,015 km:
- Simple linear average H: 3,316 km → f = c/(4H) = 22.6 Hz
- Area-weighted average H: 2,135 km → f = 35.1 Hz
- Pole-only H: 8,537 km → f = 8.8 Hz
Our claimed '~22 Hz (H_eff ≈ 3,400 km)' uses a slightly rounded simple linear average. The actual computation gives 3,316 km → 22.6 Hz, which rounds to ~22 Hz. This is accurate. The pole-only value (8.8 Hz) is the closest to observed 7.83 Hz but still 12% too high, and using pole-only height for a flat-disc cavity resonance is physically unjustifiable — the resonance involves the entire cavity, not just the tallest point.

Note: the 2.8× ratio in the Cycle 1 review (22/7.83 = 2.81) is correct for the simple-average method. Using area-weighted averaging, the ratio is 4.49×. Either way, the dome's baseline prediction overshoots the observed frequency.

**V6 Cross-Reference Check:**
The text references 'Section 2.1' — this is correct V6 numbering (old Section 4.5.1, Schumann self-contradiction in the Self-Contradictions tab). The href (#part2) and onclick (showTab 'selftest') are correct.

**Cross-WIN Consistency:**
WIN-061 is grouped with WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063 (geomagnetic monitoring cluster). The Schumann-specific cluster (002, 029, 038, 061) all consistently cite the ~22 Hz baseline prediction and the H(r) formula. WIN-002, WIN-029, WIN-038, and WIN-061 all carry the Self-Contradicted verdict. Internal consistency is good — no contradictions between our arguments across these WINs.

The WIN-038 stability tension (±0.3 Hz claimed vs 0.3–1.0 Hz storm shifts) remains the strongest unexploited argument. This is a genuine logical trap: if both WIN-038 and WIN-061 are 'confirmed,' the dome claims Schumann is both stable (WIN-038) and unstable (WIN-061) during the same events.

**Severity Assessment:**
No critical issues. The moderate holes (imprecise citation, unused 'Unscored' dashboard evidence) are real but not credibility-threatening — a dome defender could quibble about the citation target but the underlying physics is solid. The minor holes (averaging method, unexploited copper argument) are optimization opportunities, not vulnerabilities.

Overall verdict confidence is HIGH (0.94). Self-Contradicted is clearly correct: the dome's geometry predicts the wrong baseline frequency, the monitoring has never validated the claim, and the proposed firmament material (copper) is physically incompatible with storm-time conductivity changes.

citations_verified: doi.org/10.1007/978-3-662-03137-4_11 — Valid. Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002, 'Resonances in the Earth-Ionosphere Cavity' (Springer). Chapter 11 covers cavity resonance theory. Authoritative but general — not specifically about storm-time suppression.
citations_unchecked: Section 2.1 cross-reference — verified at V6 structural level (correct anchor, correct tab). Content accuracy depends on SEC-2.1 review (still pending in tracker).
